name: First Harvest
description_short: First Harvest Credit Union is a New Jersey-based credit union offering personal and business banking, lending, and investment services with community-focused member support.
description_long:
First Harvest Credit Union operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving communities across New Jersey. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, it operates under the "people helping people" philosophy and emphasizes personalized service and community investment. The organization contributed over $85,000 to community partners in 2025 and paid $5.5 million in dividends to members, demonstrating its commitment to returning value to its membership.

The credit union offers comprehensive financial products across personal and business banking. Personal services include checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, educational savings plans, auto loans, personal loans, credit builder loans, student loans, mortgage loans, home equity loans, and credit cards. Business members can access business checking, business savings accounts, non-profit accounts, and business loans. Additional services include digital banking, bill pay, debt protection insurance, investment advisory services, retirement planning, and estate planning consultations.

First Harvest distinguishes itself through multiple accessibility and member service features. The institution offers a Virtual Branch with video chat capabilities for face-to-face remote banking, allows account opening in minutes, and provides flexible loan application processes. The company emphasizes inclusive banking with personalized member service and operates financial education programs including MoneyEDU (free online learning platform), financial reality fairs, calculators, and educational videos. Their current promotional rates include 3.00% APY on growth savings, 2.00% APY on growth checking, 4.99% APY on auto loans, and 3.50% APY on 8-month share/IRA certificates.

First Harvest is a solid mid-sized credit union with established operations, physical branch locations, and legitimate regulatory standing (routing number 236077755). The main limitation is that membership typically requires affiliation with specific employers, community groups, or geographic areas—not all consumers can join. Rates and terms appear competitive but should be compared against other credit unions and banks for individual circumstances.
services: Certificates of deposit and IRA accounts
Auto loans (new and used vehicle financing, refinancing)
Personal loans and credit builder loans
Mortgage loans and home equity loans
Business checking and business savings accounts
Investment advisory and retirement planning services
pros: Member-owned structure with $5.5M+ in annual dividend payouts to members
Virtual Branch with video chat capabilities for remote member service
Competitive rates: 3.00% APY savings, 4.99% auto loans, 3.50% 8-month CDs
Credit builder loans available to help establish or rebuild credit
Comprehensive financial education platform (MoneyEDU) at no cost
Diverse product range including mortgages, home equity, student loans, business accounts
Strong community commitment with documented charitable contributions and 300+ events attended annually
cons: Credit union membership restricted by employer, group affiliation, or geographic location—not universally accessible
Limited branch network (serves primarily New Jersey markets)
No mention of mobile app capabilities on website despite digital banking offerings
No specific information about overdraft policies, NSF fees, or minimum balance requirements provided
Loan approval and rate qualification dependent on credit score and membership eligibility
best_for: New Jersey residents and workers who qualify for membership seeking personalized service and competitive rates
Small business owners and non-profits requiring business banking with community-focused service
Credit builders and consumers working to establish or rebuild credit history
Employees of member organizations or community groups seeking member-owned banking benefits
diagnosis: First Harvest Credit Union is best for New Jersey-area members who qualify for membership and seek a community-focused financial institution with competitive rates, comprehensive lending products, and strong member education resources. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted and not universally available—individuals must verify they meet the credit union's field of membership requirements before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: First Harvest — Credit Union in DEPTFORD, NJ | CreditDoc
meta_description: First Harvest — NCUA-insured credit union in DEPTFORD, NJ. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
